creator: llaquet@protonmail.com
text:
I'm using packages.

On my machine, everything works fine while using FreeBSD 14.0 (the last subversion, p9 I think), but once I upgrade to 14.1, when I try to start any VM I get the error "Kernel driver not installed (rc=-1908)" on a message window and the virtual machine doesn't start.

I tried uninstalling the packages emulators/virtualbox-ose and emulators/virtualbox-ose-kmod and reinstalling them, then restarting my machine, but it doesn't solve the problem.

I've noticed that after installing the packages, it says the following:

To avoid crashes due to kernel incompatibility, this module will only
load on FreeBSD 14.0 kernels.

I don't know if that means anything.

My /boot/loader.conf, groups and users are configured correctly, according to the instructions. The fact that everything is working fine on v14.0 I guess that proves that.

If executed the command 
# sysctl -a
And the following messages might be relevant:
...skipping...
vboxdrv: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX VMMR0.r0
vboxdrv: XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX VBoxDDR0.r0
...skipping..
KLD vboxdrv.ko: depends on kernel - not available or version mismatch
<118>/etc/rc: WARNING: Can't load vboxdrv module.

Thank you very much for your time and work and please forgive me if I'm doing anything wrong or my lack of knowledge is wasting your time.
